metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Amend 8f43 AC to require a named synchronization mechanism (e.g., a named pipe/event, a lock-file seam, or a barrier) that guarantees the first acquirer holds the lock when the second acquirer attempts it.
findings.finding: Child 8f43-b034-d2b9-4d7f describes a cross-process lock-identity test that must show 'a second acquirer via a symlink blocks until release' but does not specify any barrier, instrumented seam, or synchronization primitive. Parent AC-3 explicitly prohibits sleep-timing races in concurrency tests.
findings.location: child 8f43-b034-d2b9-4d7f, Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Use a multiprocessing.Barrier or a side-channel file (e.g., an 'acquired' sentinel written by the first process and polled by the test harness before launching the second) to guarantee ordering without sleep.

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a consumer-impact analysis to df7b: enumerate all callers of _acquire_fcntl (or its wrapping acquire path), verify each handles immediate OSError (or LockTimeout subclasses), and update callers or the exception hierarchy accordingly.
findings.finding: Child df7b-fa4c-2af5-495a changes `_acquire_fcntl` to raise immediately on non-contention OSErrors (ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF) instead of waiting and returning the timeout sentinel. No child analyzes the impact on existing callers that currently expect `LockTimeout` (or the -1 sentinel) for all lock-acquisition failures and may not handle immediate OSError raises.
findings.location: child df7b-fa4c-2af5-495a (What, Acceptance Criteria)
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a new exception subclass (e.g., `LockDeviceError(LockTimeout)`) for non-contention OSErrors so existing `except LockTimeout` callers still catch them, while allowing discriminating callers to distinguish contention from device failure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a doc entry (e.g., a section in CONTRIBUTING.md, a test-patterns ADR, or an inline README in the test directory) that defines the barrier/instrumented-seam pattern, shows a minimal reference example, and is referenced from the AC so future contributors can find and follow it.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new mandatory testing pattern — barrier/instrumented seams for deterministic concurrency tests — as an Acceptance Criterion, but provides no documentation or ADR entry to guide future contributors who must write or maintain such tests.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet: 'Newly-added concurrency/race tests are deterministic (barrier/instrumented seams, not sleep-timing races…)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'make typecheck green' to the acceptance criteria of every child that modifies production source (df83, df7b, 8368, e4cf, 799f, b1ea, df83, 8111, eba0, 6b2c).
findings.finding: The parent SC-4 requires 'make lint && make typecheck && make test' to stay green, but only child b1ea-0f03 references 'make lint' and no child references 'make typecheck'. Children that introduce production code changes (df83-e54d, df7b-fa4c, 8368-9909, e4cf-dfce, 799f-3937) carry only 'make test green' in their acceptance criteria, leaving type-checking compliance unenforced across the changed set.
findings.location: Parent SC-4; children df83-e54d, df7b-fa4c, 8368-9909, e4cf-dfce, 799f-3937 (Acceptance Criteria sections)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend each affected child's AC to read: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test green.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify 8f43's target: either specify a new file name (e.g., `test_lock_canonical_tracker.py`) or explicitly coordinate with 2202 on shared fixture isolation in test_lock_stale_reclaim.py.
findings.finding: Children 2202-923d-dbb7-49a6 (adds table-driven tests to `tests/unit/test_lock_stale_reclaim.py`) and 8f43-b034-d2b9-4d7f (adds a cross-process lock-identity test 'near' the same file) have overlapping ownership of the same test location with no coordination. If 8f43 adds to the same file rather than a new file, shared fixtures or tmpdir state could collide.
findings.location: child 2202-923d-dbb7-49a6 (Scope); child 8f43-b034-d2b9-4d7f (Scope)
findings.suggested_fix: Have 8f43 create a new file `tests/unit/test_lock_canonical_tracker.py` to avoid any fixture sharing with 2202's table-driven tests.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short spike/probe task (or a RED-fixture step in the first concurrency child story) that confirms a testable seam exists in the index.lock reclaim and detached-HEAD paths before committing to the barrier-based approach.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that deterministic concurrency tests will be achieved via 'barrier/instrumented seams' without a prior spike or RED fixture confirming those seams are achievable in the existing test infrastructure for the TOCTOU (index.lock) and detached-HEAD paths.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Every P0 finding (index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU; detached-HEAD local-ahead preservation) has a deterministic behavioral regression test'; Acceptance Criteria — 'Newly-added concurrency/race tests are deterministic (barrier/instrumented seams, not sleep-timing races)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-task: 'Spike: locate or introduce injectable seam in index.lock reclaim path — verify a thread-barrier can trigger the TOCTOU window in a unit test without sleep; timebox 2h, outcome gates the concurrency story.'  If the seam does not exist, the hardening step must add it explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation task to the epic that captures the 'justified structural test' policy (categories: git-argv adapters, import layering, config-as-artifact, runtime artifacts) in CLAUDE.md or an ADR so future contributors can self-classify without re-running an audit.
findings.finding: The plan establishes a consequential, nuanced policy — distinguishing ~59 'justified' structural/source-inspecting tests from ~9 'anti-pattern' ones — but does not plan to capture this decision in any durable artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or contributing guide) for future test authors.
findings.location: Context section — '59 of 77 source-inspecting tests are JUSTIFIED … This epic only touches the ~9 ANTI-PATTERN and the handful of BORDERLINE cases'; Success Criteria — 'Each change-detector regression test named by the audit is either replaced … or explicitly re-justified'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a child story: 'Document structural-test classification policy in CLAUDE.md (or docs/adr/NNN-structural-test-policy.md): enumerate the justified categories, the anti-pattern signal, and the re-justification process — reference this epic as context.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] (Advisory) Consider grouping the 19 children into labeled subsystem clusters (e.g., git-lock, security/YAML, review-UX, docs) in the epic description so reviewers and contributors can orient quickly without parsing all 19 independently.
findings.finding: The 19 children span genuinely unrelated subsystems (git-lock TOCTOU, YAML security-rule validation, completion-review token counting, read-freshness facades, symlink/realpath lock identity, docs, change-detector heuristics, Jira inventory, etc.) — a diffusion pattern that is advisory-only per rubric but worth surfacing because it raises scope-certainty risk: a contributor picking up any single child must orient across a wide, unrelated solution space, and a regression in one subsystem is hard to catch in review of another.
findings.location: Decomposition state — full child list

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for the behavioral-test-replacement approach: e.g., 'We replace rather than add a mutation-testing gate because the false-confidence is in existing tests that would pass mutation testing trivially (they inspect structure, not behavior); behavioral replacements give immediate CI signal and are readable as specifications. Property-based testing for concurrency paths is deferred — the barrier/seam approach is sufficient for the known failure modes and keeps the suite deterministic on CI runners with variable timing.'
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for the chosen approach of behavioral-test replacement over alternatives that differ structurally. Two plausible alternatives exist: (A) mutation testing (e.g., mutmut/cosmic-ray) applied to the identified anti-pattern tests to automatically verify they fail on relevant mutations — a structural defense that doesn't require manual per-test rewrite decisions; (B) property-based testing (Hypothesis) for the concurrency paths, which would generate edge cases the handcrafted barrier tests might miss. The plan asserts the behavioral-replacement approach without explaining why it is preferred (codebase alignment, blast-radius, simplicity, robustness) — the absence of positive rationale is a G6 finding.
findings.location: Context section ('This epic converts the false-confidence regressions…'); Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering constraint or coordination note between 6b2c and e4cf regarding their shared ownership of test_code_review_ws5.py; alternatively, split the file into two separate test modules.
findings.finding: Children 6b2c-69b0-7729-4422 and e4cf-dfce-4166-4215 both make distinct structural changes to the same file, `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py`, with no stated coordination, ordering, or merge strategy, creating a high-probability merge conflict and risk that one child silently reverts the other's changes.
findings.location: child 6b2c-69b0-7729-4422 (Scope); child e4cf-dfce-4166-4215 (Scope)
findings.suggested_fix: State that e4cf lands after 6b2c (or vice versa) and include a 'rebase on sibling' note; or move e4cf's path-contract tests to a new `test_detector_path_scope.py` to eliminate the shared-file conflict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that df83 lands before bdbe (or vice versa) and that bdbe rebases on df83's gitutil.py changes; document the interface contract between the reclaim function and the retry seam.
findings.finding: Children df83-e54d-1637-47ae and bdbe-be66-ff34-48dd both modify `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` — df83 hardens `_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock` and bdbe adds a seam to `_with_index_lock_retry` — with no stated ordering, sequencing, or merge coordination between them.
findings.location: child df83-e54d-1637-47ae (Scope); child bdbe-be66-ff34-48dd (Scope)
findings.suggested_fix: Sequence df83 first (it changes the reclaim logic) and have bdbe explicitly rebase on df83's gitutil.py changes; add a note in bdbe's scope referencing the df83 inode-revalidation API.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit dependency: b1ea must land and stabilize the lock.py structure before df7b adds errno branching; or coordinate both changes in a single commit sequence with shared ownership.
findings.finding: Children b1ea-0f03-c4ec-40f7 (dual_window lifecycle — may remove or restructure lock.py) and df7b-fa4c-2af5-495a (add errno branching to _acquire_fcntl in the same lock.py) both modify `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` with no stated ordering or coordination. If b1ea removes the dual-window path, the surrounding code context for _acquire_fcntl changes, requiring df7b to rework its diff.
findings.location: child b1ea-0f03-c4ec-40f7 (Scope); child df7b-fa4c-2af5-495a (Scope)
findings.suggested_fix: Mark df7b as 'depends on b1ea' in its description; have df7b's author rebase on b1ea's final lock.py state before submitting.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to 7f1b's scope: also update the code_review-exclusion entry in docs/coverage.md to cite the new behavioral test introduced by 003d; or add this to 003d's AC.
findings.finding: Child 003d-e6b0-9e58-4b52 adds a behavioral test proving `code_review` tickets are excluded from Jira sync, while child 7f1b-fb57-edcc-4385 updates the coverage matrix in `docs/coverage.md`. Neither child references the other, so 7f1b's matrix update may land without citing the new behavioral test from 003d, leaving the matrix entry for `code_review` Jira exclusion either absent or pointing only to the old structural test.
findings.location: child 003d-e6b0-9e58-4b52 (Acceptance Criteria: 'code_review Jira exclusion is proven by running reconcile'); child 7f1b-fb57-edcc-4385 (What, Acceptance Criteria)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend 7f1b's AC with: 'The code_review Jira-exclusion entry cites the behavioral reconcile test from 003d, not a source-grep assertion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cross-cutting AC to the epic (or a shared CI linting step) that scans all new/modified test files in the changeset for hasattr-only assertions, inspect.getsource calls, and bare constant-equality assertions on private names, and fails the gate if found.
findings.finding: Parent AC-2 ('No test in the changed set asserts only hasattr/private constant/source substring where the underlying behavior is executable') is cross-cutting across all 19 children, but enforcement is siloed: each child only audits its own named files. Children adding new test modules (8368-9909, 799f-3937, eba0-91bb, 6b2c-69b0, b6c5-3461, 8f43-b034) could themselves introduce the anti-pattern with no gate catching it.
findings.location: Parent Acceptance Criteria (AC-2); children 8368-9909, 799f-3937, eba0-91bb, 6b2c-69b0, b6c5-3461, 8f43-b034 (Acceptance Criteria)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-commit or CI step (e.g., a custom pylint plugin or a grep-based gate in the Makefile) that flags `hasattr(`, `inspect.getsource(`, and `== _[A-Z_]+` patterns in test files added/modified in the changeset.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete pointer (repo-relative path, PR link, or copied finding list) to the audit report so an executor can identify every test in scope without external investigation.
findings.finding: The audit report that defines the full remediation scope is cited by commit SHA only ('external test-coverage audit, static, pinned to commit 2d1524ad8') with no path, URL, or file reference, leaving an executor unable to locate the specific list of anti-pattern tests and borderline cases without external investigation.
findings.location: Context section, sentence: 'An external test-coverage audit (static, pinned to commit 2d1524ad8)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'Audit report: `docs/audits/test-coverage-2d1524ad8.md` (or the equivalent path)' and, if that document is short, inline the ~9 anti-pattern test names directly in this plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'handful of BORDERLINE cases' with a specific count or enumerated list (or an explicit 'borderlines are out of scope for this epic') so the executor has an unambiguous scope boundary.
findings.finding: 'Handful of BORDERLINE cases' is an undefined scope boundary — the executor cannot determine which borderline tests are in scope for this epic without the audit report, and 'handful' gives no actionable bound.
findings.location: Context section, last sentence: 'This epic only touches the ~9 ANTI-PATTERN and the handful of BORDERLINE cases'
findings.suggested_fix: Either inline the list of borderline tests to be addressed, or state a default: 'Borderline cases are excluded from this epic unless explicitly listed in child stories.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'the production path is hardened where the race is real' with a specific observable outcome, e.g., 'the index.lock reclaim path uses an atomic compare-and-delete (or equivalent) so that two concurrent reclaimers cannot both succeed' — or name the exact production function and the guard to add.
findings.finding: 'The production path is hardened where the race is real' is not stated as a specific observable outcome — 'hardened' has no defined completion state and 'where the race is real' defers a design determination to the executor.
findings.location: Success Criteria, bullet 1: '…and the production path is hardened where the race is real.'
findings.suggested_fix: State the concrete code-level change: e.g., 'GitAdapter.reclaim_stale_lock uses os.open(O_CREAT|O_EXCL) or equivalent to make the TOCTOU window unreachable, verified by a test that injects concurrent reclaimers via a barrier.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each production-path hardening step (index.lock TOCTOU; detached-HEAD preservation), add a plan section that describes the mechanism of the change, whether it alters any observable interface/error contract, and provides an explicit rollback or back-out plan (or expand-contract sequencing if callers exist).
findings.finding: The two production-path hardening steps (index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU; detached-HEAD local-ahead preservation) are named in the Success Criteria but the plan never describes what the hardening actually does — no mention of whether it changes an observable interface, data shape, or error contract, and no rollback/back-out plan if the hardening introduces a regression.
findings.location: Success Criteria — first bullet: 'the production path is hardened where the race is real'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-section under each P0 story (or in the epic body) that answers: (1) what exactly changes in the production path (e.g., 'add advisory lock retry with exponential backoff, no interface change'), (2) whether any caller-visible contract changes, and (3) the back-out steps if the hardening must be reverted (e.g., 'revert commit X; no data migration needed because change is purely additive').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each production path being hardened (index.lock reclaim, detached-HEAD preservation), add a brief note naming known callers/dependents and confirming the fix is backward-compatible or calling out required caller updates.
findings.finding: The plan adds 'hardening two thin production paths' beyond remediating test-coverage findings, but does not acknowledge callers or dependents of the hardened paths that may rely on current (racy) behavior — if the TOCTOU fix changes observable semantics (e.g., error codes, lock-reclaim timing), downstream consumers are silently affected.
findings.location: Context section: 'hardens two thin production paths the audit exposed'; Success Criteria P0 bullet
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Context' or a new 'Compatibility' subsection, list the two hardened paths with their current observable contract (return type, exceptions thrown, side effects) and state explicitly whether the fix preserves or changes that contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named stakeholder (e.g., 'engineers relying on CI to catch behavioral regressions') and at least one concrete before/after validation metric (e.g., 'N change-detector tests converted; behavioral test count increases by M') to the Success or Acceptance Criteria.
findings.finding: No named human stakeholder or team is identified as the beneficiary of this work, and no concrete before/after validation mechanism (e.g., count of behavioral vs. structural tests, a workflow comparison, or an operational metric) is provided — the AC is purely structural (CI green + no-hasattr check), making it hard to confirm the promised value was delivered.
findings.location: Success Criteria and Acceptance Criteria sections
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend a 'Beneficiary' line to Success Criteria naming the team/role and a baseline count of change-detector tests being remediated, so 'done' is measurable (e.g., 'Before: 9 anti-pattern tests; After: 0 anti-pattern tests, 9+ behavioral equivalents added').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit sequencing or priority ordering in the epic (e.g., P0 hardening children `sundried-bonny-sloth`, `archaic-elegant-bovine`, `feathered-formal-drongo` ship before or gate the broader test-replacement wave) so the riskiest production changes de-risk first rather than landing in a big-bang parallel merge.
findings.finding: The epic presents all 19 children as undifferentiated parallel open work with no explicit sequencing, despite the Success Criteria calling out P0 findings (index.lock TOCTOU hardening in `sundried-bonny-sloth`; detached-HEAD preservation in `archaic-elegant-bovine`) that involve production code changes and carry real regression risk if landed after the lower-risk test-only replacements destabilize assumptions about current behavior.
findings.location: Success Criteria section; decomposition state (all 19 children listed as 'open' with no ordering)
findings.suggested_fix: In the epic's Success Criteria or a new 'Sequencing' section, mark the P0 hardening children (sundried-bonny-sloth, archaic-elegant-bovine, feathered-formal-drongo) as Phase 1 prerequisites; group pure test-replacement children as Phase 2; and doc-only children (narrow-bairnly-kusimanse, sublime-phlegmy-oropendola) as Phase 3 that can run in parallel with Phase 2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the production-path hardening mechanism for the index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU: since git writes index.lock (rebar cannot stamp it), either (a) document that the race is accepted and the hardening is a tighter threshold + test, or (b) describe an alternative mechanism (e.g., try/except on os.remove treating ENOENT as success and EPERM/EBUSY as a live-lock signal), and confirm the behavioral regression test is written against the hardened invariant, not just the existing heuristic.
findings.finding: The plan names 'index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU' as a P0 production-path hardening target, but the actual TOCTOU in gitutil._reclaim_if_stale_index_lock (mtime stat → conditional os.remove, with no atomicity guarantee between the two) cannot be fully closed with the same pid-stamp mechanism used for the mkdir lock — git writes index.lock, not rebar, so rebar cannot stamp it. The plan gives no mechanism for what 'hardening' means here, leaving the implementer to either (a) add a test without changing production behavior, or (b) change behavior without a clear correctness argument.
findings.location: Success Criteria — P0 findings ('index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU'); src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to the P0 hardening description that states: 'The index.lock reclaim TOCTOU cannot be fully closed (git owns the file protocol); the hardening is: (1) catch OSError from os.remove and treat only ENOENT as benign; (2) add a behavioral test that confirms a lock file whose mtime races from young-to-old during the reclaim window is NOT double-removed (via monkeypatch of os.path.getmtime and os.remove). Re-justify as defense-in-depth rather than a full race closure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan a concrete fixture description for the detached-HEAD local-ahead regression test: create a tracker in which HEAD is a detached commit (git checkout --detach HEAD), commit one new event file so HEAD advances past the branch ref, then call sync.reconverge and assert the detached commit survives (is reachable from HEAD after reconverge) — confirming local-ahead detection uses HEAD not refs/heads/tickets.
findings.finding: The plan's Success Criteria requires a 'deterministic behavioral regression test' for the detached-HEAD local-ahead preservation gap, but gives no description of the test mechanism — a gap explicitly acknowledged in docs/concurrency.md ('no dedicated automated regression test in the current suite') but not addressed mechanically in the plan.
findings.location: Success Criteria — P0 findings ('detached-HEAD local-ahead preservation'); docs/concurrency.md lines 249–257
findings.suggested_fix: In the P0 section add: 'Test fixture: init a tracker with one event commit on branch tickets; detach HEAD (git checkout --detach HEAD); append a second event commit (HEAD now ahead of refs/heads/tickets); wire origin/tickets at the first commit only; call sync.reconverge; assert the second commit is still reachable from HEAD (was not reset away).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
